title: Produce first CIViC-derived graph slice (gene-variant-drug-disease)
context: With the CIViC ETL pipeline in place (oncogene-kg-extract-civic-001), the first actual data slice must be produced and published. This slice covers gene-variant-drug-disease associations from CIViC, exported as KGX and JSON-LD, with 100% provenance and passing all CI gates. Source-conflicting statements must be retained with separate provenance, not flattened. This is the first tangible deliverable of the project.
objective: Run the CIViC ETL pipeline to produce and publish the first graph data slice in KGX and JSON-LD formats, with 100% assertion provenance, passing all CI gates, and retaining source-conflicting statements with separate provenance records.
acceptanceCriteria: First slice published as KGX (TSV+JSON) and JSON-LD.
100% of assertions carry resolvable provenance (source, record ID, license, version, PMID, method, commit).
Passes CI (Biolink/SHACL + provenance-completeness + allow-list linter) before review.
Source-conflicting statements are retained with separate provenance records, not flattened to a single truth.
Dataset is committed with a version label and checksum of the input CIViC release.
A brief data README describes the slice, entity counts, and how to load it.
output: data/civic/ directory containing the KGX and JSON-LD graph slice files with checksums and a data README, committed via PR with CI green.
